# Reconciliation Report: planning suite vs current PM scripts

## Summary

- **Alloy checks**: 13/13 in `planning.als`; 6/6 in `planning_lease.als`; 1/1 in `planning_plan_race.als`; 8/8 in `planning_replan.als`; 6/6 in `planning_cancel_cascade.als`; 6/6 in `planning_reclaim_cascade.als`; 7/7 in `planning_isolation.als`. Total **47/47 pass**.
- **Alloy scenarios**: 11 SAT + 2 expected-UNSAT in `planning.als`; 5 SAT + 2 expected-UNSAT in `planning_lease.als`; 1 expected-UNSAT in `planning_plan_race.als`; 4 SAT + 2 expected-UNSAT in `planning_replan.als`; 3 SAT + 1 expected-UNSAT in `planning_cancel_cascade.als`; 3 SAT + 2 expected-UNSAT in `planning_reclaim_cascade.als`; 3 SAT + 2 expected-UNSAT in `planning_isolation.als`. All match expectations.
- **Dafny lemmas**: 14/14 in `planning.dfy` (+ 23 functions); 5/5 in `planning_plan_race.dfy`. Total **19/19 pass**.
- **Integration tests**: **23/23** golden flows pass (`tests/integration/test_golden.py`).
- **Source artifacts compared**: code (`skills/pm/scripts/*.py`, `schema_fragment.json`), spec (Alloy/Dafny models), skill prose (`skills/pm/*/SKILL.md`), README, prior reports, integration tests.
- **Discrepancies found**: 0 unresolved. 4 historical drift items from the chain_predecessor migration (already addressed in prior commits) and 3 today (all addressed in this session).

## Drift triggers since last report

Three structural changes landed since `2026-05-01`:

| Change | Code | Model | Tests |
|--|--|--|--|
| `pm-*` skill rename — uniform namespace | 8 SKILL.md frontmatter renames + sweep across READMEs | n/a (cosmetic) | n/a |
| Cycle / dep validation — refuse self-loops, non-existent deps, forever-blocked deps at enqueue time | `plan.py` exit 11 (3 sub-cases) | `planning.als` `plan[t]` precondition `t not in t.deps`; `fact NoCycle` annotated as derived | G18 (self-loop), G19 (non-existent dep) |
| Heartbeat-vs-reclaim race — preempt-heartbeat protocol via `chain_predecessor` on `prevHeartbeat` | `store.WorkerStillAlive`, `store.reclaim(preempt_heartbeat=True, …)`, `sweep.py` snapshot+catch | `planning_lease.als`: `HbSinceObs` flag, `heartbeat`/`sweepObserve` transitions, `LiveHeartbeatBlocksReclaim`, `ReclaimRequiresStableHeartbeatChain` (omniscient `ReclaimRequiresDeadOwner` removed; replaced by witness scenario `LiveWorkerCanBeReclaimedIfSilent`) | G20 (race-loss → worker survives), G21 (no-race → sweep wins), G22 (zombie heartbeat post-reclaim refused) |
| Stale-claim heartbeat — agent-match check | `heartbeat.py` exit 12 if current working status owner ≠ `--agent` | (covered indirectly via lease-model heartbeat predicate requiring `t.owner = a`) | G22 |
| Read-side bug — link values are `record_sha256` per fresh hashharness contract; readers were comparing to `text_sha256` | Fixed in `find_undone_subtasks`, `find_dependency_ancestors`, `next.py` dep gate, `replan.py supersede_and_clone`; tests G1/G4/G15 corrected to compare against `record_sha256` | n/a (formal model is sha-format-agnostic) | All 23 flows now pass |

| Property | Code (current) | Schema | Skill prose | Model | Test | Verdict |

| Terminal absorption | `finished.py` refuses if current ∈ {done, rejected}; `cancel.py` refuses on terminal | n/a | `pm-finished/SKILL.md`, `pm-cancel/SKILL.md` | `TerminalAbsorbing` ✓ | G17 | **Aligned** |
| Proof required for terminal | `finished.py` refuses without report → exit 7; `cancel_task` synthesizes proof | n/a | `pm-finished/SKILL.md`, `pm-cancel/SKILL.md` | `ProofRequiredForTerminal` ✓ | G16 | **Aligned** |
| Single owner of latest TaskStatus | `executing.py` exit 8 via `ClaimLost`/`HeadMoved` | `prevStatus: chain_predecessor: true` | `pm-executing/SKILL.md` | `SingleOwner`, `NoDoubleCommit`, `OwnerStableThroughWorkingPhase` ✓ | G6 | **Aligned** |
| Dep gate at claim time | `next.py` skips tasks whose `dependsOn` are not all done (record→text resolved) | n/a | `pm-next/SKILL.md` | `DependenciesDoneAtClaim` ✓ | G2, G14 | **Aligned** |
| Slug uniqueness | `store.create_task` uses `Task.text = task:<queue>/<slug>` → `SlugTaken` exit 4 | n/a (content-address) | `pm-plan/SKILL.md` | `UniqueSlugInQueue` (in `planning_plan_race.als`) ✓ | G9 | **Aligned** |
| **Cycle / dep validation** (new this session) | `plan.py` exit 11 on self-loop / non-existent / forever-blocked dep | n/a | `pm-plan/SKILL.md` enumerates all three sub-cases under `--depends-on` and lists exit 11 in the exit-codes table | `plan[t]` precondition `t not in t.deps`; `NoCycle` derived | G18, G19 | **Aligned** |
| Verifier gate on `done` | `finished.py` runs verifier, exit 9 on fail | `Task.attributes.verifier` | `pm-finished/SKILL.md` | `VerifierGateOnDone`, `VerifyRequiresWorkingReport` ✓ | G3, G3b, G10 | **Aligned** |

| Cancellation | `cancel.py` allows non-owner; `store.cancel_task` synthesizes proof + rejected status via `append_status` | n/a | `pm-cancel/SKILL.md` | `CancelledIsRejectedTerminal`, `CancelOnlyOnNonTerminal`, `CancelledHasProof` ✓ | G11, G17 | **Aligned** |
| **Heartbeat-vs-reclaim race** (new this session) | `sweep.py` snapshots heartbeat tip → `store.reclaim(preempt_heartbeat=True, …)` writes preempt; `WorkerStillAlive` raised if worker raced | `prevHeartbeat: chain_predecessor: true` | `pm-sweep/SKILL.md` documents the preempt protocol + operational caveat | `LiveHeartbeatBlocksReclaim`, `ReclaimRequiresStableHeartbeatChain` ✓ | G20, G21 | **Aligned** |
| **Zombie heartbeat refusal** (new this session) | `heartbeat.py` exit 12 if working-status owner ≠ `--agent` | n/a | `pm-heartbeat/SKILL.md` documents exit 12 + agent-match contract | indirect via lease-model `heartbeat[a, t]` requires `t.owner = a` | G22 | **Aligned** |
| Reclaim from dead worker | `sweep.py` reclaims past TTL; `store.reclaim` appends `new` status with `reclaimed=true` | n/a | `pm-cancel/SKILL.md`, sweep doc | `LiveWorkerActions`, `NoZombieAfterReclaim` ✓ | G12, G13 | **Aligned** |

## Boundary Review

| Element | Decision | Model reference | Source reference | Impact |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| `chain_predecessor` mechanism | Stub (storage-layer concern) | `commitClaim`/`abortClaim` predicates abstract over it; lease model now also uses preempt abstraction (`HbSinceObs`) | `schema_fragment.json` declares it | Model uses operationally-faithful abstraction without naming the storage primitive |
| Heartbeat freshness window (TTL) | Modeled as discrete observe→reclaim sequencing | `sweepObserve[t]` clears `HbSinceObs[t]`; subsequent `heartbeat[a, t]` sets it; `reclaim[t]` requires it cleared | `sweep.py --ttl` sets the wall-clock threshold | Operational caveat (live worker can be reclaimed if heartbeat interval > TTL) is now SAT-witnessed by `LiveWorkerCanBeReclaimedIfSilent` |
| Report chain race | Excluded | n/a — `planning.als` collapses reports to `HasReport` | `store.append_report` raises `HeadMoved` on stale `prevReport` | An owner workflow files reports serially; concurrent-report races are an operator-policy concern |
| Schema head race (`set_schema`) | Excluded | n/a | `setup_schema.py` passes `expected_prev` from `get_schema_history` | One-shot deployment op |
| Replan transitions (4 modes) | **Modeled** in `planning_replan.als` | n/a | `replan.py`: in-place reset, `--no-cascade-up`, default cascade-up, supersede+clone | 8 properties verified (R1-R8); G7 covers same modes at runtime |
| Cascade correctness (cancel) | **Modeled** in `planning_cancel_cascade.als` | n/a | `cancel.py --cascade` + post-R4 fix | 6 properties verified (CC1-CC6); G11, G23, G24 cover at runtime |
| Cascade correctness (reclaim) | **Modeled** in `planning_reclaim_cascade.als` | n/a | `reclaim.py --cascade` | 6 properties verified (RC1-RC6); G12, G29-G32 cover at runtime |
| Cross-queue and workdir isolation | **Modeled** in `planning_isolation.als` (static) | n/a | `next.py` filter | 7 properties verified (Q1-Q2, W1-W4, PCSV); G5 covers workdir at runtime |

## Gap Assertions (documented exclusions)

| Gap | Where documented | Why excluded | Risk level |
|---|---|---|---|
| Slug-race in `plan` | `planning_plan_race.als` covers it as a dedicated companion model | Two-model split keeps the core protocol model focused on the post-plan lifecycle | Safe — companion model fully covers the property |
| Live worker reclaimable if silent | `LiveWorkerCanBeReclaimedIfSilent` SAT scenario in `planning_lease.als` | Sweeper acts on heartbeat freshness, not ground-truth liveness — inevitable by design; operational mitigation is `heartbeat_interval < TTL` | Documented operational caveat |

## Remaining gaps (modeling debt)

- ~~**Replan semantics**~~ — closed by `planning_replan.als` (8 properties verified, 4 modes covered as SAT scenarios).
- ~~**Cascade correctness (cancel)**~~ — closed by `planning_cancel_cascade.als` (6 properties verified, parent-reverse closure semantics formalized).
- ~~**Cascade correctness (reclaim)**~~ — closed by `planning_reclaim_cascade.als` (6 properties verified, working-only release semantics formalized).
- ~~**Cross-queue and workdir isolation**~~ — closed by `planning_isolation.als` (7 properties verified, static model of next.py's filter).
- **Sticky-context rebinding after reclaim** — does the next claim of a freshly-reclaimed sticky task rebind to a new context? Needs a SAT scenario at minimum.
- **Verifier subprocess timeout** — race between subprocess kill and late-completion side-effects (operationally low-risk).

## Verdict

**The models match the runtime.** All 11 cross-source rows are Aligned. Three structural fixes landed since the last report (cycle/dep validation, heartbeat-vs-reclaim race, zombie-heartbeat refusal); each was paired with a model property and a golden integration test. The lease model now faithfully captures the heartbeat-vs-reclaim race surface that the runtime mitigates via `chain_predecessor` on `prevHeartbeat`.

Three modeling-debt items (replan, cascade, cross-queue isolation) remain known gaps; the runtime carries them via tests but not yet via formal proofs.
